chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ بَعْدَ أَنْ يُغَسَّلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – What comes out from the deceased after having been washed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/333)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/333)
text: إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ شَيْ‏ءٌ بَعْدَ مَا يُكَفَّنُ فَأَصَابَ الْكَفَنَ قُرِضَ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of his companions,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When something comes out from the deceased after having been shrouded, and it hits the shroud, it should be cut off from it’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If after shrouding a certain part of it (cloth) becomes unclean, it must be cut off and disposed.’”
